title: How every Sweet 16 game was won Thursday -- and each winner's keys to Elite Eight
content: Arizona, Illinois, Iowa and Purdue are moving on. Here's how they advanced -- and how they can do it again.

title: Arizona rolls, hands Calipari worst tourney loss
content: Arizona finally got over the Sweet 16 hurdle under coach Tommy Lloyd, getting 23 points from Brayden Burries and a dominant offensive effort in a 109-88 win over Arkansas on Thursday night in the NCAA tournament.

title: Tucker getting into groove as Dodgers win opener
content: Kyle Tucker padded the Dodgers' lead as Los Angeles drubbed the Diamondbacks to begin their three-peat quest.

title: Phils' Bohm accuses parents of siphoning money
content: Phillies third baseman Alec Bohm has sued his parents for millions of dollars, alleging they moved large amounts of his money into financial accounts they managed for him and then used some of the cash to pay their own expenses.

title: Gervin irked QB Williams filed 'Iceman' trademark
content: Former NBA star George Gervin said he was "caught off guard" when he learned Bears quarterback Caleb Williams had attempted to trademark "Iceman," a nickname Gervin has used since his playing days.

title: Illini defense gets tough to oust No. 2 Houston
content: Illinois held Houston to just 34% shooting in its lowest-scoring game of the season, as the Fighting Illini won 65-55 to advance to the Elite Eight.

title: Huskers' Hoiberg shoulders personnel blunder
content: Coach Fred Hoiberg said a late-game blunder that left his team with only four players on the court before a critical three-point play in the final minute of Thursday night's loss to Iowa in the Sweet 16 was his fault.

title: Kaufman-Renn's tip-in sends Purdue into Elite 8
content: Trey Kaufman-Renn tipped in a bucket with 0.7 seconds left, giving Purdue a 79-77 win over Texas in the Sweet 16 of the NCAA tournament.

title: 🏈 NFL draft: Biggest needs for all 32 teams
content: Our NFL reporters listed the top three positional needs for all 32 teams ahead of April's draft, and our draft experts found non-Round 1 fits to fill them.

title: Predicting the 2026 season, from the playoffs and World Series to MVP and Cy Young
content: Can the Dodgers three-peat? Will Skubal and Skenes win another Cy Young? Our experts make their predictions.

title: Updated NBA rookie rankings: Why the ROY race is all but over
content: Where do the NBA's top rookies stack up with a few weeks left in the regular season? We stacked the top 10.

title: 'It's all or nothing': UCLA veterans ready for final NCAA title run
content: Lauren Betts and her teammates have unfinished business since falling short at the Final Four a year ago.

title: Lunardi: Want Cinderella back? Set an NCAA tournament eligibility floor
content: The blame for Cinderella's demise is misplaced. Here's why we haven't seen her -- and how we can get her back.

title: Spiky and unforgettable, Wales went 'full gas' but paid the price
content: Wales scrapped for every inch of the pitch against Bosnia and Herzegovina, but in the end, it wasn't enough to save their World Cup dream.

title: World Cup playoff preview, predictions: Who will book the final six spots?
content: The final six spots -- four from Europe and two from the intercontinental playoff -- will be clinched this week.

title: How Arbeloa has won over doubters and reinvigorated Real Madrid's season
content: Whether it's the coach, the players, or both, Real Madrid have improved since Álvaro Arbeloa took over in January, and he could yet save their season.

title: Ranking coaches and teams that are likely to improve in 2026
content: Curt Cignetti reset how we view coaching turnarounds while Oklahoma State, with a new coach, has nowhere to go but up.

title: An early look at 2027 NFL free agency: Which playmakers could be available next March?
content: Which top players might be available in next year's NFL free agency? We picked out 49 stars to keep an eye on.

title: Ranking all 32 free agency classes: Which teams most improved their rosters?
content: How did every team fare in March, taking into account free agent signings, trades, extensions and departures?

title: The unexpected path that led Morgan and Frankie Price to Arkansas
content: The Price sisters have led the Arkansas gymnastics team in their final season of the sport -- and they hope it ends with an NCAA championship berth.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, tips, strategy, stats and more
content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the fantasy baseball season.

title: 'The KING ... Shot by ME': Caitlin Clark photographs LeBron James at Lakers-Pacers
content: The Fever star was behind the lens as a credentialed photographer at Wednesday's Lakers-Pacers matchup.

title: Each MLB team's most interesting giveaway this season
content: Not all ballpark giveaways are created equal. Here's each team's most creative offering for the upcoming campaign.

title: Women's tournament: UConn still title favorite as Virginia upset shakes up the odds
content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: 'What I'm thinking is arena football but outdoors': How Mike Repole is remaking the UFL
content: Repole is the man behind many of the UFL's changes, including smaller venues and the four-point field goal.

title: 25 trends to know before draft season begins
content: Mike Clay shares his favorite notes from the research that informed his fantasy projections for 2026.

title: 'Definitely not my final chapter': A refreshed Israel Adesanya isn't done yet
content: He may have traded right hooks and calf kicks for DJ decks last year, but Israel Adesanya insists he is motivated to continue his UFC career -- starting this weekend against Joe Pyfer.

title: Max Verstappen vs. F1's new rules: Every complaint this season -- and our verdict
content: Amid a wave of complaints from F1's four-time world champion Max Verstappen, ESPN's Nate Saunders examines every one and gives his verdict on if they are warranted.

title: From IndyCar royalty to NASCAR dynasty: How Penske conquered American racing
content: Roger Penske's racing enterprises have long been synonymous with IndyCar, but now they've built the most decorated team in recent NASCAR history, too.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal in for PSG star Kvaratskhelia
content: Arsenal are exploring an approach for Paris Saint-Germain winger Khvicha Kvaratskhelia. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Watch: Massive ruckus in J&K Assembly as NC MLAs protest over killing of Iran's Ali Khamenei
content: National Conference MLAs protested the killing of former Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei and US-Israel military actions in Iran within the Jammu and Kashmir Assembly. The protest led to a scuffle with BJP MLAs, prompting an adjournment. Chief Minister Omar Abdullah condemned the conflict, stating regime change cannot be achieved through military bombardment.

title: Did CIA tell Donald Trump Iran’s supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei is gay? What US President claimed
content: President Trump stated the CIA informed him Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is gay, a claim he believes "a lot of people are saying." This revelation, reportedly met with laughter during an intelligence briefing, comes amid reports of a long-term relationship with his tutor and past travel for impotence treatment.

title: Embarrassment for PSL as Labuschagne left baffled by low quality jersey turning ball pink
content: The Pakistan Super League began its 2026 season with an unusual incident. During the opening match between Lahore Qalandars and Hyderabad Kingsmen, the white ball turned pink. Dye from the Hyderabad Kingsmen's jerseys rubbed off onto the ball. This unexpected color change caused concern and drew attention on social media. The match proceeded with the altered ball.

title: Anthropic thanks judge blocking Pentagon from declaring it 'national security risk'
content: A federal judge has temporarily blocked the Pentagon from labeling AI giant Anthropic a "national security risk." The ruling halts a presidential directive and a defense secretary's designation, allowing Anthropic to continue business with defense contractors. The judge criticized the government's actions as an "attempt to cripple Anthropic" rather than a direct response to security concerns.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' grosses Rs 805 crore in India; worldwide total nears Rs 1,100 crore
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is a box office sensation, crossing Rs 800 crore gross in India within its first week. The film, directed by Adiya Dhar, has shown remarkable performance, earning an estimated Rs 674.17 crore net. Overseas collections also stand strong at Rs 261.92 crore, nearing its predecessor's international success.

title: Indore techie killing: Chilling CCTV video shows sequence of events as teen mowed down woman
content: A dispute over an Airbnb rental in Indore tragically turned deadly when an 18-year-old allegedly rammed a car into residents, killing a software engineer and injuring others. CCTV footage captured the horrifying incident within a gated township. The violence erupted after residents voiced concerns about frequent outsiders entering the complex due to short-term rentals, leading to a confrontation.

title: Get rid of old assumptions: Microsoft HR makes it clear to employees that it's time to change
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman has announced a significant restructuring of the HR division, prioritizing adaptability and speed to align with the company's AI-first strategy. This overhaul includes leadership changes and the formation of new teams focused on accelerating HR operations, shaping culture, and fostering workforce acceleration.

title: US brings GARC drone boats into Iran conflict — why it matters
content: America has deployed uncrewed drone speedboats for patrols against Iran, marking a significant first in active conflict. These "Global Autonomous Reconnaissance Craft" (GARC) are capable of surveillance and potentially strike missions.  The Pentagon confirmed their use in "Operation Epic Fury," highlighting their extensive operational hours and miles logged, underscoring a new era in naval warfare.

title: To keep fuel prices stable, govt hikes ATF duty, cuts excise on petrol, diesel
content: Amidst the Middle East conflict impacting global oil supplies, India has implemented measures to ensure domestic fuel availability. The government has imposed export duties on diesel and aviation fuel while simultaneously reducing excise duties on petrol and diesel for local consumption, aiming to shield citizens from price hikes.

title: When 13-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a 157kph ball for straight six
content: A single, audacious six off extreme pace in a trial session catapulted Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into the cricketing spotlight.  The teenage prodigy, who turned 15 in March 27, 2026, has since dominated age-group and senior cricket, even smashing a 35-ball century in IPL 2025.  Rajasthan Royals secured him for Rs 1.1 crore, a move that has already proven its worth.

title: Top executives of two biggest US companies are leaving and they say the reason is AI
content: Two prominent CEOs, from Coca-Cola and Walmart, have cited the rise of AI as a factor in their decisions to step down. Both leaders indicated that the evolving business landscape, driven by AI, necessitates new leadership for future growth phases. Their departures highlight a significant technological shift impacting executive decision-making at major corporations.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 goes below 23,000; BSE Sensex down over 1,000 points
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex dropped in opening trade on Friday as US-Iran war tensions and crude oil prices continued to weight on sentiment. While Nifty50 went below 23,100, BSE Sensex was down over 800 points.

title: Peddi teaser release: Ram Charan unveils gritty wrestler avatar
content: Ram Charan unveiled a powerful teaser for his upcoming sports drama, 'Peddi,' on his birthday. The gripping visuals showcase his raw, rugged wrestler persona, complete with intense training sequences and a formidable physique.  The film, directed by Buchi Babu Sana, also stars Janhvi Kapoor and features music by AR Rahman, promising a compelling narrative set for a 2026 release.

title: GPS disruptions, mine warnings: How Indian sailors in Hormuz are navigating Gulf tensions
content: Indian seafarers face severe GPS disruptions and warnings of underwater mines while navigating the Strait of Hormuz. Repeated radio broadcasts from Iran's IRGC Navy forbid passage, forcing reliance on manual navigation in the volatile and dangerous waterway. The situation is unprecedented, with flammable cargo on many vessels amplifying the risks.

title: Magic Johnson opens up on living with HIV years after diagnosis
content: Magic Johnson recently revisited his journey living with HIV during a conference in Atlanta, offering a candid reflection shaped by decades of experience. From his shocking 1991 retirement to becoming a global advocate, Johnson turned fear into purpose. Supported by his wife Cookie and guided by medical progress, he continues to inspire through honesty, education, and resilience, proving his impact extends far beyond basketball.

title: NFL Trade rumor: Chargers linked to $90 m Dolphins star to boost Justin Herbert offense
content: Tyreek Hill trade talk picked up again as the Los Angeles Chargers are reportedly targeting Miami Dolphins playmaker Tyreek Hill, a $90M talent, to address their offensive struggles. A viral post suggested that Hill could join the Chargers or Chiefs after the NFL Draft. Money also plays a role. Hill may not command the full value after the injury. That makes this a smart gamble for Los Angeles. If the Chargers pull this off after the draft, it solves their biggest issue. It adds speed, forces defences to back off, and finally gives Herbert the weapon he has been missing.

title: MLB Rumors: Detroit Tigers' $140 m call as Kevin McGonigle rises
content: Detroit's decision to place Kevin McGonigle on the Opening Day roster has created a roster crunch, pushing Javier Báez into an uncertain role.  With his substantial contract, trade talks are intensifying. A potential deal with Miami could see Báez move for prospects, offering Detroit payroll relief and a clearer path for younger talent.

title: MP horror: Man kills wife during car journey, dumps body while baby sleeps in rear seat
content: A 25-year-old woman was found strangled in the Pench river, with police revealing her husband killed her during a car journey. The couple's two-year-old child was present in the vehicle when the crime occurred. The accused allegedly dumped the body to conceal the murder.

title: Pressure, trolling, captaincy: Riyan Parag faces his biggest IPL yet
content: Riyan Parag steps into IPL 2026 as Rajasthan Royals captain amid intense scrutiny and past trolling. Backed by the franchise after a rigorous selection process, the 24-year-old faces a defining season. Inspired by Hardik Pandya’s journey and focused on team success, Parag aims to silence critics and revive his chances of returning to the Indian team.

title: 'They asked for 7, I gave 10': Trump on Iran's 'big present' to US as Hormuz deadline delayed
content: President Trump granted Iran a 10-day cooling-off period, calling it a "gift" after Tehran allowed eight oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. This pause in planned strikes on energy infrastructure comes amid ongoing diplomatic efforts, which Trump described as progressing well. The extension follows a previous five-day delay and a prior 48-hour ultimatum.

title: New Covid variant with 75 mutations raises concerns: Doctor says stay alert, do not panic
content: A new COVID-19 variant with multiple spike protein mutations has emerged, showing signs of immune escape. While this raises concerns, experts emphasize that the situation differs significantly from the pandemic's start due to widespread hybrid immunity and improved healthcare preparedness. The virus is now considered endemic, behaving like other respiratory illnesses.

title: Delhi School result 2025-26 for Classes 6, 7, 8 to be released tomorrow
content: The Directorate of Education (DoE), Delhi, will release the school results for Classes 6, 7, and 8 tomorrow, March 28, 2026. Students can check their scores online at edudel.nic.in by entering their Student ID and Date of Birth. The official website will host the results for students to download and print.

title: Govt cuts excise duty to Rs 3 a litre on petrol, to nil on diesel amid Middle East tensions
content: India has reduced its fuel duty, lowering the special additional excise duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre and removing it entirely on diesel. This move comes amid global oil supply disruptions and follows a price hike by Nayara Energy. Crude prices retreated as US-Iran negotiations showed progress.

title: Meet Patrick Winston: MIT lecture that stayed packed for 40 years
content: Patrick Winston's "How to Speak" lecture at MIT, a decades-long tradition, emphasized practical communication skills for technical professionals. The session, now accessible online, taught structured presentations, the "empowerment promise," memorable idea techniques, and impactful conclusions, proving vital for conveying complex information effectively.

title: Ram Navami 2026: SS Rajamouli and team 'Varanasi' wish fans; shares Ramayana scene
content: Filmmaker SS Rajamouli's 'Varanasi', starring Mahesh Babu as Lord Ram, offered a glimpse of an epic battle sequence on Ram Navami, teasing a 2027 release.  The film promises a grand scale with a significant battle scene.  Meanwhile, Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana' and Bhavna Talwar's 'Valmiki Ramayana' also shared updates, with the latter unveiling its first poster.

title: Burned with cigarettes, nail driven into leg: Gaza toddler tortured by Israeli forces
content: Israeli soldiers reportedly tortured a one-year-old child in central Gaza. The child, Karim, allegedly suffered burns and puncture wounds. This happened while his father, Osama Abu Nassar, was interrogated. The child was later returned to his family. His father remains in detention. The family seeks international intervention for his release.

title: Indian women’s football’s freefall: Five coaches in five years, another one loading
content: India’s disastrous AFC Women’s Asia Cup campaign, marked by three losses, including a humiliating 0–11 defeat to Japan, has exposed the All India Football Federation’s chronic mismanagement. Frequent coaching changes, rushed appointments, and opaque decision-making reflect a lack of planning, leaving the team directionless.

title: Rupee hits record low past Rs 94 per US dollar amid Iran war
content: Rupee hit a fresh record low of 94.1550 against the US dollar on Friday, extending its losing streak amid persistent foreign institutional investor outflows and ongoing Middle East tensions. Despite supportive cues like falling crude oil prices and a weaker dollar, the currency continued its downward trend, reflecting a broader market sentiment of risk aversion.

title: ‘Residents cut power to penthouse’: Moments before Indore teen rammed car into crowd
content: An 18-year-old boy rammed his car into residents in Indore after a dispute over an Airbnb-rented penthouse escalated. The incident tragically killed a woman software engineer and injured several others. Police have registered murder and attempted murder charges against the accused and are investigating Airbnb visitor logs.

title: PSL opener sees ball turn 'pink', fans flood social media with memes - Watch
content: Lahore Qalandars kicked off the Pakistan Super League 2026 with a dominant win, but the match was overshadowed by a bizarre incident where the ball inexplicably turned pink.  Despite the unusual occurrence and broadcast glitches, the focus remains on the cricket and the viral online buzz surrounding the unexpected colour change.

title: Attempt to save injured dog turns violent in Faridabad; rescuer critical
content: Animal welfare volunteers were attacked in Faridabad's Dabua Colony while attempting to rescue an injured dog. One accused, Sanjeev alias Gora, has been arrested after being identified through security footage. The incident resulted in serious injuries to a male volunteer and alleged molestation of a female volunteer.

title: IPL schedule 2026: Full list of fixtures for all 10 teams
content: The BCCI has unveiled the IPL 2026 schedule, with the 19th season commencing on March 28 and concluding on May 31. A total of 74 matches will be played, featuring a home and away format for each of the 10 teams. The top four teams will advance to the playoffs after the league stage.

title: Over 1 million fighters mobilised: How Iran is preparing for possible US ground invasion
content: Iran has reportedly mobilised over a million fighters, including military personnel and volunteers, amid escalating tensions with the US and Israel. This comes as the Pentagon develops plans for potential ground troop deployment, though President Trump has publicly denied such intentions while leaving the option open. US allies are also being urged to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Iran oil revenue soars as others struggle due to Hormuz crisis; how much is it earning daily?
content: Iran is experiencing a dual benefit from rising oil prices, with daily revenue increasing significantly in March. Despite ongoing tensions, crude exports remain near pre-war levels, with activity at Kharg Island terminal picking up. Iran has also blocked ships to aggressor nations via the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 'Talks going very well': Trump pauses strikes on Iran energy infra for 10 more days
content: President Trump has extended the deadline for potential strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure by ten days to April 6, 2026, citing a request from Tehran and positive negotiation progress. This move follows multiple extensions of an ultimatum regarding the Strait of Hormuz, with US envoy Steve Witkoff indicating strong signs of Iranian openness to talks.

title: Unfinished business: Shreyas Iyer’s Punjab Kings target IPL 2026 glory
content: Punjab Kings enter the new IPL season with renewed purpose after a narrow six-run loss in last year's final. Led by skipper Shreyas Iyer, the team boasts a balanced squad with a strong Indian core, including explosive openers Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya. They aim to leverage their experience and a potent all-rounders' department to finally clinch their maiden title.

title: First since 1861: US paper currency to bear Donald Trump’s signature
content: The Trump administration is proposing to feature the President's signature on US dollar bills for the nation's 250th anniversary. Officials cite economic achievements and national pride as reasons. This move, however, faces scrutiny due to historical laws restricting living individuals on currency and a separate plan for a Trump commemorative coin.

title: 10, including 9 from one family, fall ill after eating momos in Noida
content: Ten people, including nine from a single family, fell ill after allegedly consuming momos in Dadri, Gautam Buddh Nagar. The individuals experienced symptoms like stomach pain, fever, and vomiting, and were treated at a local health center. Samples from the shop have been collected for testing.

title: India, China neighbours that can't be parted: Beijing envoy
content: China's envoy to India, Xu Feihong, asserted that India and China's progress stems from their people's efforts, not external aid, subtly countering US remarks. He urged win-win cooperation and cautioned against those seeking to sow discord between the neighbours. Xu also highlighted the need for China and India to champion developing nations amidst rising global challenges.

title: Oil prices fall as Trump pauses attacks on Iranian energy plants, Brent at $105
content: Oil prices are soaring past $100 a barrel as the Middle East conflict nears a month, impacting global supply. Brent crude is trading above $107, while WTI is near $94.  Goldman Sachs predicts further increases before a gradual decline.  Iran's tightening control over the Strait of Hormuz and potential new fees on ships are key factors driving these price hikes.

title: Late Night Meet With PM Modi: Inside Story On Excise Duty Cut On Fuel
content: Sources said the Prime Minister reviewed the situation and directed officials to ensure that any surge in international oil prices does not burden the common people.

title: To Be Nepal PM Balen Shah Raps In First Post-Election Message
content: Nepal's soon-to-be Prime Minister has delivered his first post-election message in the form of a rap, which has taken the internet by storm.

title: Opinion: Opinion: Sarabjit Singh, Kulbhushan Jadhav, And A Dhurandhar Question
content: Ranveer Singh's portrayal of an Indian spy in Dhurandhar feels, in many ways, like a tribute, not merely to the cinematic idea of espionage, but to the real men and women who serve India in silence.

title: Maharashtra Man Arrested For Raping Woman, Claimed He Was God
content: The woman gathered the courage to file a complaint against the 40-year-old accused after the arrest of self-styled godman Ashok Kharat in Nashik.

title: "Can't Issue Ultimatum": Iran's Speaker's Warning After Trump's War Pause
content: The statement coincided with a shift in US policy, as Donald Trump extended the temporary halt on planned strikes on Iranian power plants, signalling ongoing diplomatic engagement alongside continued military pressure.

title: Rumours Of Lockdown In India Completely False, No Such Proposal: Hardeep Singh Puri
content: Amid rumours of a lockdown in India, Hardeep Puri asked citizens to "remain calm, responsible, and united".

title: Iran Military Gets Chipmaking Tech From China's Top Supplier: US Officials
content: SMIC, China's largest chipmaker, has sent chipmaking tools to Iran's military, two senior Trump administration officials said on

title: Opinion: Iran's Missile War: What The '90% Degraded' Headline Misses
content: Within the first ten days, open-source assessments suggest that between 60 and 90 per cent of Iran's MRBM inventory had been fired or destroyed.

title: Taking Kharg Island Could Expose US Troops To 'Drones And Mines'
content: President Donald Trump has been weighing whether to use ground forces to seize Iran's strategic oil hub of Kharg Island, an operation analysts say

title: A Owaisi-Led AIMIM To Campaign For Badruddin Ajmal's AIUDF In Assam
content: On April 2 and 3, Owaisi will address several public meetings in Assam to campaign for AIUDF candidates, including for Badruddin Ajmal.

title: US Government Sued For Revealing Identities Of Around 100 Epstein Survivors
content: Survivors of late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein sued the US government and Google on Thursday over victims' identities being mistakenly revealed in a trove of documents published...

title: Rupee Hits Record Low At 94.29, Rs 7 Lakh Crore Wiped Off Markets In An Hour
content: As oil becomes expensive, India needs more dollars to pay for imports. At the same time, global investors are rushing to the safety of the US dollar

title: Excise Duty Cut On Petrol, Diesel: Check Latest Fuel Prices In Your City
content: Following the revision, central taxes on petrol have come down to Rs 3 per litre, while diesel now attracts zero excise duty.

title: Israel Calls Report Claiming Its Forces Burnt Gaza Toddler With Cigarettes "Propaganda"
content: According to the report, the toddler, identified as Karim, was detained with his father, Osama Abu Nassar, near the Al-Maghazi refugee camp. Footage aired by the broadcaster showed visible injuries on the child's body.

title: "A Bad Start For Him": Trump On Reports Of Mojtaba Khamenei Being Gay
content: A report in the New York Post had claimed that Donald Trump was "stunned" to learn that US intelligence indicated that Mojtaba Khamenei may be gay.

title: Doctor Found Dead In Car With Cannula In Arm, Department Head Under Probe
content: The doctor, a native of Ambala, Haryana, was found unconscious early Wednesday morning by her father inside her car.

title: Iran War LIVE: Centre Cuts Excise Duty On Petrol, Diesel To "Ensure Citizens Are Protected"
content: The war in the Middle East began when the US and Israel conducted strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other senior leaders.

title: 'No Thanks': Trump Claims No One In Iran Wants To Be The Supreme Leader
content: Trump also insisted that Tehran was negotiating with Washington to end the war, despite Tehran's denial.

title: Parliament panel calls for preserving British-era heritage sites not under ASI ambit
content: The committee has recommended that the Ministry of Culture "prepare an inventory of non-ASI heritage structures at risk, in coordination with state governments" and also ensure protection of the architectural heritage component, under the proposed new infrastructure scheme, specifically addressing 100-200 year-old facades and structures

title: The Transgender Persons Act and the question of identity: Who gets to define a trans life?
content: As India tightens its transgender law, love, desire, and dating apps reveal how trans people are still asked for proof before acceptance

title: Haryana’s cotton farms holding on by a thread
content: The Bt cotton revolution of the early 2000s had farmers in Haryana’s Hisar, Sirsa, Fatehabad, and Jind reaping rewards. Now, the pink bollworm is slashing yields and squeezing profits and the MSP is not enough. As farmers ditch cotton for paddy, the impact is soon likely to be felt by farm labourers, traders, and the economy as a whole, reports Ashok Kumar

title: Watch: Explained: The faultlines in DMK’s seat-sharing
content: The DMK wants to contest around 175 seats and cross the majority mark on its own. Chief Minister MK Stalin has insisted that everything is smooth within the alliance. Parties like MNM and VCK have publicly backed the DMK. Though Stalin remains confident, the seat sharing has exposed an undercurrent.

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: Former BJP T.N. president Annamalai unhappy with constituencies allocated by AIADMK
content: A Trinamool Congress worker has been shot dead in Kolkata ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections; Kolkata Police have arrested four people in connection with the killing

title: Kerala polls: Expelled IUML leader Irshad arrested, released on bail over derogatory remarks against woman MLA
content: According to police, a case was registered against Irshad based on a complaint by U. Prathibha. He was taken into custody, and his arrest was recorded. As the charges invoked were bailable, he was released on bail

title: Tunnel road is most viable solution to Bengaluru’s traffic congestion: D.K. Shivakumar
content: D.K. Shivakumar estimates that around ₹3,000 crore be will required for land acquisition and ₹17,000 crore for tunnel road construction

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026 | No women, no vote: movement calls for NOTA revolt
content: Thulya Prathinidhya Prasthanam urges voters to back women candidates or opt for NOTA, slams fronts for ‘systematic exclusion’

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Congress expels rebel KPCC member in Taliparamba
content: Koyyam Janardhanan had openly opposed the UDF’s decision to support T.K. Govindan, a former CPI(M) leader contesting as an Independent with UDF backing in Taliparamba

title: Palghar ‘godman’ booked for rape; complainant mustered courage after Kharat case
content: Police said the woman, a Pune resident, first came into contact with the accused through Facebook in 2023.

title: In a first, multi-layered security and holding areas introduced at Bengaluru’s M. Chinnaswamy Stadium
content: On Thursday, the State Expert Committee overseeing the overhaul of the stadium conducted a final drill and formally announced that the venue has undergone all upgrades satisfactorily

title: Kerala polls: K.C. Venugopal accuses Pinarayi Vijayan of targeting Rahul Gandhi, being silent on Modi
content: AICC general secretary alleges Kerala CM is not speaking against PM Modi ‘as he had signed agreements’ with the Centre on PM SHRI and labour codes

title: India sends medical supplies to Afghanistan
content: Pakistan's airstrike on Kabul on March 16 killed around 400 people and injured more than 250. India has "unequivocally" condemned the attack

title: Cabinet approves ₹33,660 crore BHAVYA scheme for 100 new industrial parks by 2032
content: While the Central government will provide funding of up to ₹1 crore per acre, the scheme is meant to include the involvement of the respective State governments as well as the private sector

title: Threat to life, says Samajwadi Party Lok Sabha MP Afzal Ansari
content: He also said selective targeting of individuals takes place through movies and individuals facing numerous cases are patronized by the Uttar Pradesh government

title: Watch: 1,033 candidates in one election: Tamil Nadu’s craziest poll
content: Today, voting in India is simple. You walk into a polling booth, press a button on an Electronic Voting Machine, and your vote is cast in seconds.  But in 1996, voters in one constituency in Tamil Nadu faced a very different situation. They had to search through a booklet running into more than 60 pages just to find the candidate they wanted to vote for. Because, that election had 1,033 candidates.

title: CAG flags gaps in Gujarat’s financial oversight; ₹7,400 crore in UCs pending
content: The CAG also found that departments reported funds as fully utilised merely upon transferring them to implementing agencies, even when the money remained unspent or was later returned

title: Banas Bio-CNG model gains national traction; Gujarat allocates ₹60 crore for expansion
content: The State government plans to set up around 10 Bio-CNG plants in phases through cooperative milk societies

title: From hills to markets: Indigenous seeds make a comeback in Andhra Pradesh
content: A grassroots network of farmers in Andhra Pradesh’s Eastern Ghats is conserving indigenous seeds and reshaping how food is grown and shared

title: INZBC launches southern India chapter in Chennai to boost India-New Zealand trade, innovation ties
content: This chapter of the India New Zealand Business Council will serve as a key hub for engagement across southern States, complementing its broader national and international network

title: Only a structured party combined with popularity can reach success: Thousand Lights MLA N. Ezhilan
content: Thousand Lights MLA from the DMK N. Ezhilan, in an interview to The Hindu, observes that any popular personality entering politics will attract the limelight. But only a structured party machinery combined with popularity can ensure success. In this interview, he speaks about his tenure as first-time MLA from Thousand Lights Assembly constituency in Chennai, Vijay’s political entry shaping the 2026 elections, redressal of key grievances and more.

title: Kerala assembly polls 2026: Though not in the fray, they are in the poll arena as campaigners
content: Some former legislators and leaders who are not in the fray this time  are still in the limelight as campigners

title: SSM College of Pharmacy and Research Centre in Vijayapura selected for NAIN programme to turn students into job providers
content: The college will establish a dedicated Innovation and incubation centre, providing students and faculty with resources, mentorship, and industry linkages to transform ideas into viable startups

title: Self-styled godman Ashok Kharat charged with case of extortion, Opposition demands strict action
content: Another case has been registered against self-styled godman and sexual assault accused Ashok Kharat, alleging cheating, instilling fear of death, taking the total to eight cases

title: Hyderabad’s rapid urban expansion: Resetting the lines of control
content: From the bustling lanes of its historic core to the glass towers of the western IT corridor and the ever-expanding suburbs beyond, Hyderabad’s rapid growth is testing its administrative limits. As the capital of India’s youngest State grows outwards, the government has overhauled its policing structure, redrawing boundaries, reshaping existing commissionerates and introducing a new one to keep pace with the city’s scale. The move aims to improve oversight and bring policing closer to residents, even as questions remain over coordination and on-ground clarity, write Lavpreet Kaur and Naveen Kumar

title: 15 women professionals term menstrual leave policy ‘benevolent sexism’, move Karnataka High Court
content: Petitioners claim that the policy creates a competitive disadvantage for women in hiring and promotions, discourages employers from placing women in leadership roles, and infringes upon privacy by compelling disclosure of menstrual cycles

title: As ‘Ladki Bahin’ spends crores, malnutrition deaths persist in Melghat, Bombay HC asks State for explanation
content: Despite orders passed by the High Court since 2007, the State had done “too little” for the welfare of tribal communities and had not succeeded in preventing deaths caused by malnutrition in the region

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026:  Bitter battle for nine seats that can turn electoral fortunes
content: The LDF, the UDF and the NDA are locked in three-cornered fights in the constituencies of Nemom, Kazhakkoottam, Vattiyoorkkavu, Aranmula, Pala, Thrissur, Nattika, Palakkad and Manjeshwaram. The outcomes from these seats could even determine the final outcome of the polls.

title: President urged not to grant assent to Transgender Amendment Bill
content: The letter was written by All-India Feminist Alliance (ALIFA), a pan-India collective of grassroots organisations, along with National Alliance for Justice, Accountability and Rights (NAJAR); the groups said they were ‘extremely alarmed and distressed at the undue and unjustifiable haste’ with which the Bill was passed in Parliament

title: The surging cost of health care in Kerala
content: The interest shown by global private equity firms in Kerala’s private health sector has given rise to apprehensions that medical treatment may become a costly affair. A.S. Jayanth finds out A look at the possible fallout of the massive foreign investments in Kerala's hospitals

title: What we know so far about the Supreme Court’s ruling on reservation for converted Dalits
content: The Supreme Court ruled that a person professing any religion other than Hinduism, Buddhism, or Sikhism cannot be recognised as a member of a Scheduled Caste

title: Andhra Pradesh electric bus: APSRTC’s electric push has staff worried
content: The State government’s drive to replace diesel buses in the APSRTC fleet with electric ones promises a cleaner and quieter future, but it has also sparked anxiety among employees, who fear that the transition to e-buses could eventually threaten job security and livelihoods, writes P. Sujatha Varma

title: DMK can release unfulfilled promises as its manifesto: Anbumani Ramadoss
content: In a statement, he claimed that 439 of the 505 promises made ahead of the 2021 Assembly elections remain unfulfilled, accusing the ruling party of misleading the public with claims of near-total implementation

title: Annamalai unhappy with constituencies allocated by AIADMK
content: The former BJP Tamil Nadu president is learnt to have requested the party leadership not to identify any seat for him as he has decided against contesting in the Assembly election. But he will  continue to work “as a party karyakarta for all BJP and NDA  candidates”

title: Rolling back rights: Karnataka’s gender minority communities question Trans Bill 2026
content: Karnataka has played a significant role in shaping India’s gender minorities rights movement, with activists, lawyers and groups laying the groundwork. Shilpa Elizabeth talks to the community that feels The Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill recently passed by  Parliament undoes some of the gains of the struggle

title: Tamil Nadu’s Nitish Kumar or assertive chief? Edappadi Palaniswami confronts a battle of perceptions
content: DMK claims AIADMK is subservient to the Modi government, framing the Assembly election as Tamil Nadu vs Delhi; BJP leaders avoid naming Mr. Palaniswami as NDA’s CM face, but he asserts himself in seat-sharing announcements

title: Parliamentary panel pitches for RPF-like force for highways
content: The parliamentary committee said a patrol service could improve real-time response on high-accident corridors and expressways during the ‘golden hour’ enforce speed discipline on access-controlled roads, and deter damage to highway infrastructure

title: India has oil for 60 days, LPG for a month: Centre
content: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said all petrol pumps across the country are adequately stocked and operating normally, with no rationing of petrol or diesel

title: Court rejects Ramadoss’ plea on PMK’s mango symbol
content: The court makes it clear that all observations are limited to deciding the interim applications and wwill not affect the final outcome of the suit.

title: NHRC issues notice to Chhattisgarh government over death of 285 inmates in different jails of State in four years
content: State government in the Assembly cited suicide and chronic ailments as the reasons behind the inmates’ death

title: Bihar will remain indebted to Nitish Kumar for building a ‘New Bihar’: Nitin Nabin

title: Bihar’s youth now making their mark in technology field: IT Minister Shreyasi Singh
content: IIT Patna Incubation Centre signed two MOUs with AIIMS Patna & JHPIEGO. IT Minister addressed 10th Foundation Day celebration of IIT Patna Incubation Centre

title: Ro-ro ferries fitted with Automatic Identification System
content: The AIS system enables the ferry master to monitor the movement of nearby vessels and crafts, thereby enhancing navigational safety

title: Liquor no more be served near Shree Jagannath Temple, Puri
content: Odisha’s new excise policy imposes 0.5% de-addiction Cess and prohibits opening of new ‘ON’ shops in rural areas

title: Woman techie killed as 18-year-old boy runs her over by car over society dispute in Indore

title: A closer look at every UFL team's new uniforms for 2026
content: the eight-team spring football league returns with three relocated franchises and a full slate of redesigned uniforms . the three-time champions will debut a gold home uniform featuring "Stallions" across the chest, while the road whites shift the focus to "Birmingham" with gold and garnet accents, the update extends to the helmet as well .

title: 'Changing of the guard': Cole Hutson poised to take big role in next wave for the Capitals
content: the 19-year-old defenseman was days out from signing his three-year, entry-level contract with the Washington Capitals . he surprised Hughes with his strength, prompting a frustrated rebuttal by Hughes via cross-check to the ice . it's early yet, but the message from Hutson was clear: this NHL stage wasn't intimidating the teen .

title: Men's tournament: Three bets and two Eliminator picks for the rest of the round of 16
content: the 2026 men's basketball tournament round of 16 is here and for my money, it's college basketball nirvana . Throughout the tournament, I will provide my best bets for each round as well as my picks for the new ESPN Men's Tournament Challenge Eliminator game . this is the best, most competitive basketball you will see all year.

title: Saudi Pro League, MLS or elsewhere: Where could Salah go next?
content: Mohamed Salah is leaving Liverpool at the end of the season after nine years . he joined the club in 2016 when they were still enduring their long wait for a league title . his impact on Liverpool can hardly be overstated, but where could Salah head next?

title: Ranking the 25 best players in the women's Sweet 16

title: UCLA coach Kelly Inouye-Perez breaking records amid changing landscape
content: Kelly Inouye-Perez is the all-time winningest coach in the history of the division I softball program . in her 20th year as head coach, she added the record to a list of accomplishments . "we are so much more than wins," she says .

title: Capitals' Ovechkin notches 34th career hat trick
content: Alex Ovechkin tallies a hat trick as the Capitals beat the Mammoth (1:01) SALT LAKE CITY --  Alex Ovechkin  had his 34th career hat trick,  Ivan Miroshnichenko  scored twice, and the  Washington Capitals  beat the  Utah Mammoth   7-4  on Thursday night to snap a two-game losing streak. Ovechkin scored his first goal at the 5:01 mark of the second period, when he tipped in a puck in traffic, cutting Utah's lead to 3-2. He tied the score at 3-3 with 8:32 left in the second, chipping the puck under the crossbar. Ovechkin completed the hat trick with an empty-netter with 5.2 seconds left. The 40-year-old passed Brett Hull for fourth-most hat tricks in NHL history. Ovechkin has scored hat tricks against 21 different franchises, passing Hull for the most in NHL history, according to ESPN Research. Anthony Beauvillier  and  Rasmus Sandin  also scored for the Capitals, while  Logan Thompson  made 35 stops for Washington. The Associated Press contributed to this report.
